description:
Adds a "Send SMS Reminder" button to the hr.applicant Action menu
(form + list). Clicking it opens a modal popup form view showing:
  - Applicant name
  - Mobile number (partner_phone)
  - Pre-filled SMS text matching the applicant's current stage
...and two action buttons in the footer:
  - SMS Sent → stamps the right x_studio_sms_*_date field and closes
  - Cancel → closes without any state change

Stage routing (same wording as the SMS Queue Digest in 002):
  - Stage 1 (New) with no attachment → resume-missing SMS, stamps
    x_studio_sms_new_reminder_date
  - Stage 2 (Qualification) → info-survey SMS, stamps
    x_studio_sms_reminder_date
  - Stage 7 (Assessment Sent) → assessment SMS, stamps
    x_studio_assessment_sms_reminder_date
  - Any other stage → UserError ("no SMS template for this stage")

Implementation: a custom form view of hr.applicant rendered in
target='new' modal mode, with the SMS text held in a new
x_studio_sms_preview (Text) field between button click and modal
render. Pure procedural Python — no closures.
